indication: Abdominal distension and weight loss in known cirrhosis; evaluate ascites and liver.
technique: Grayscale and Doppler ultrasound of the abdomen.
findings: Coarse, nodular, shrunken liver consistent with cirrhosis. Splenomegaly (16 cm). NEW moderate-volume ascites. Portal vein patent with hepatopetal flow; features of portal hypertension. No discrete focal liver mass identified on this limited ED study. Gallbladder/biliary tree unremarkable.
impression: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension and new ascites. No focal liver lesion seen on this study — dedicated multiphase liver imaging for HCC surveillance had been planned in the outpatient weight-loss workup.
comparison: No recent cross-sectional liver imaging on file.
